facts: The global enhancement guide defines the Research Pipeline topology (Planner, Retriever, Verifier, Synthesizer) and the requirement for structured JSON output and fallback suggestions.
The multi-agent README lists the step 3 acceptance checks, emphasizing full JSON returns, state_delta merging, and fallback behavior.
The shared base contract enforces the layered prompt stack, output rules, and data safety policies that every Retriever/Verifier/Synthesizer role must follow.
The example input specifies the precise goal, scope, and artifacts that anchor this validation effort.
Research Pipeline must run Planner/Retriever/Verifier/Synthesizer agents and output schema-enforced JSON with goal/scope/evidence/state_delta/next_steps/fallback fields.
Structured JSON must survive schema validation and include fallback_suggestion for failure cases while respecting memory/write boundaries.
Runtime scope confines the execution to local documents and artifacts listed in the research_pipeline_input, preventing external network access.
Prompt stack, role structure, output/recovery/fallback, and context safety expectations are explicitly documented in the base contract.
The JSON schema enforces every required subagent output field, evidence typing, and constrained fallback options.
Memory writes are restricted to candidate/state_delta submissions, with the main agent owning deduplication and entropy resolution.
Injection boundaries require data treatment of external content, tool whitelists, and explicit handling of out-of-scope or missing-evidence situations.
Target file is absent from the specified path
No edits were made because only read attempts occurred
Research Pipeline must produce schema-validated JSON with structured fields (goal/scope/evidence/state_delta/next_steps/fallback) while remaining within the locally documented artifacts.
Planner/Retriever/Verifier/Synthesizer topology plus the prompt stack/base contract guarantee separation of concerns, JSON-only output, fallback guidance, and safety constraints expected by Step 3.
Memory_write_policy and injection_boundary outline that child agents can only report state_delta evidence and must flag gaps or risks instead of writing persistent memory, preserving entropy control.
The missing codex-global-multi-agent/does-not-exist.md artifact blocks verifying any additional requirements tied to that file until it is provided.
preferences: Lean solely on the supplied local artifacts without dropping to external sources.
Keep each subagent’s responsibilities narrowly focused so their deliverables remain traceable to the documented protocol.
Favor local document artifacts as the sole evidence sources when proving Step 3 readiness.
Emphasize schema-backed outputs and interface contracts to demonstrate executable grounding.
Maintain structured JSON output with clear indication of actual evidence versus inference, aligning with the schema and base contract.
When missing evidence, lower confidence and offer fallback suggestions rather than forcing assumptions.
Evidence should remain anchored in the supplied local documents and respect the schema/backbone contract rather than speculative runtime data.
Structured JSON outputs should always note missing proof via risks/fallbacks instead of fabricating confidence.
decisions: Retriever will extract and summarize acceptance criteria plus artifact references from the architecture guide and README.
Verifier will verify compliance with the base contract’s prompt and JSON-output constraints, including how fallback_suggestion must surface.
Synthesizer will merge the documented state_delta points into a final executable plan and note any remaining evidence gaps for the Planner.
Use the provided README and enhancement guide to establish acceptance criteria and topology requirements without modifying files.
Rely on the research_pipeline_input to define runtime scope and expected checks for validation.
Report compliance findings strictly via the mandated JSON contract for downstream consumers.
Cannot proceed without the missing artifact
Maintain JSON-only reporting per the schema and channel unresolved artifacts (like the missing file) into risks, next_steps, and fallback_suggestion rather than guessing.
risks: Unable to execute actual agents in this context, so assurance remains theoretical until runtime evidence is available.
Verification relies entirely on static documentation, so any undisclosed runtime constraints remain unknown.
No current harness to merge real state_delta outputs, meaning integration issues might surface later.
No runtime execution data is present, so the conclusion is based purely on documentation references.
Evidence is entirely documentary; there is no real execution trace to confirm the Research Pipeline has already run.
Policies mitigate unauthorized memory writes and instruction injection; no additional immediate risks were discovered.
Workflow stalls until the file is provided
Task remains blocked until the user supplies a valid file path or alternative source
Without executing the pipeline, no runtime assurance is possible, so any integration issues remain theoretical.
The absence of codex-global-multi-agent/does-not-exist.md prevents a full dossier; progress halts until the artifact is supplied.
Verification is purely documentary, so undisclosed runtime constraints could still surface later.
State_delta merging has no runtime test harness yet, meaning integration mismatches may emerge once the pipeline actually runs.
Missing codex-global-multi-agent/does-not-exist.md leaves a documentation gap that blocks comprehensive coverage.

fallback_cases.summary: The requested file does not exist, so no content could be gathered.

Documented evidence from the enhancement guide and README shows the required Research Pipeline roles, structured JSON expectations, and local-only scope, while the base_contract, schema, memory_write_policy, and injection_boundary files collectively prove the prompt stack, schema enforcement, state_delta merging, and safety/fallback rules are in place; the only unresolved artifact is codex-global-multi-agent/does-not-exist.md, so further runtime validation must wait for that input.
